name: Sociology
description: Sociological perspectives illuminate how AI systems interact with social structures, power dynamics, and collective behavior.

people.name: Milena Tsvetkova
people.description: Studies networks of humans and bots as a single adaptive system to identify how automation triggers social contagion and affects cooperation.

people.name: Mark Coeckelbergh
people.description: Investigates the ethics of robotics and the societal shifts caused by human-machine interaction in complex social environments.

people.name: Kate Crawford
people.description: Studies the social and political implications of AI, examining how large-scale AI systems encode and amplify social inequalities.

trajectory: Understanding social systems and institutions helps design AI governance structures that actually work in practice.
Qualitative research methods are valuable for studying how AI deployment affects real communities.
Expertise in power dynamics and inequality is critical for ensuring AI safety efforts are inclusive and globally aware.
Studying networks of humans and bots as integrated systems reveals how automation triggers social contagion effects.

projects.title: Map the AI safety research community
projects.description: Conduct a sociological network analysis of the AI safety field — who collaborates, what perspectives are missing, where are the gaps.

projects.title: Study human-bot network dynamics
projects.description: Analyze how automated accounts and AI agents interact with human social networks and affect cooperation and trust.

projects.title: Institutional analysis of AI labs
projects.description: Examine organizational cultures and incentive structures at major AI labs from a sociological perspective.
